card.intuition: A way to run JavaScript in a separate background thread, allowing you to perform heavy calculations without freezing the main UI thread.
card.analogy: A restaurant with a separate prep kitchen: the main chef (UI Thread) handles the plating and customers, while a 'Prep Cook' (Web Worker) chops 50kg of onions in the back room so the dining room doesn't slow down.
when_to_use: Image or audio processing in the browser
gotchas: Web Workers have NO access to the DOM, `window`, or `document` objects
Communication between the main thread and workers is done via an event-based system (`postMessage`) and data is cloned, not shared

steps.annotation: Main thread: runs all UI code. Blocking work here freezes the page.
new Worker("worker.js") creates a background thread with its own JS runtime.
Worker has no DOM access. Ideal for CPU-intensive work: parsing, image processing, crypto.
main.postMessage(data) — data is structured-cloned (deep copy) to the worker.
worker.postMessage(result) — result flows back. Main thread updates UI with the result.

exercise.prompt: Complete the logic for sending data to a worker and listening for a response.
hints: You 'post' a message and then handle the 'on' message event
exercise.test_code:
def check(pm, om): return pm == "postMessage" and om == "onmessage"
assert check("postMessage", "onmessage")
print("PASS")
